forecasts.reasoning: To resolve NO, the word "tariff" or "tariffs" must appear on the specified URLs for The New York Times OR the Wall Street Journal *every single day* for a 43-day period (May 19 to July 1). Sustaining a 43-day streak in news headlines/blurbs for a specific policy tool like tariffs is incredibly difficult, given news cycles naturally shift to other topics like foreign conflicts, domestic politics, natural disasters, or holidays where business news is light. Weekends (especially Sundays, when the WSJ does not print a new edition) introduce severe bottlenecks where the streak would rely entirely on the NYT's coverage. Unless a paper introduces a permanent front-page widget (like a "Tariff Tracker"), it's virtually guaranteed there will be at least one day without the word in both URLs. Therefore, the chance of a YES resolution (at least one day without the word) is very high.
